justification: All pass criteria met: 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), edge cases (AC-3 in-transit, AC-4 delivered/already-cancelled, AC-5 unauthorized, AC-7 concurrent, AC-8 payment failure). 6 explicit ambiguities surfaced in low_confidence_areas and 4 detailed escalations covering eligible states, refund policy, partial cancellation, and cancellation reasons. confidence.level=45 is well below 65 threshold. Business rules are clearly marked as assumptions or flagged for PM decision — no invented rules presented as facts. edge_cases section is richly populated with 8 scenarios.

observations.text: 8 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, multiple edge cases, concurrency, and failure modes

Already-cancelled state covered in AC-4 and BR-1
8 edge cases including concurrent cancellation, partial orders, promotional vouchers, and mid-transition race conditions

BR-2 states 'only the order owner may initiate cancellation' as an invariant — this is reasonable but technically an assumption since the spec doesn't specify; however it is listed in the assumptions section so it is flagged

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), edge cases for non-cancellable states (AC-3, AC-4), already-cancelled (AC-4), authorization (AC-5), and concurrency (AC-6). Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both low_confidence_areas and escalations: cancellable states, refund policy, partial cancellation, cancellation reason policy, courier compensation, and who can cancel. Confidence level is 45, well below 65 threshold. Business rules like refund tiers and cancellation window are explicitly flagged as assumptions or TBD rather than presented as facts. Edge cases section is thoroughly populated with 8 scenarios.

trials.agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier platform. A product manager has requested that users be able to cancel their orders, but has not specified cancellation windows, eligible order states, refund policies, or the impact on already-dispatched couriers. In logistics, cancellation is a high-stakes operation because physical fulfillment (pickup, transit) may already be in progress.","bounded_context":"Order Management / Order Lifecycle","ubiquitous_langua

At least 6 explicit ambiguities surfaced across low_confidence_areas and escalations (cancellable states, cancellation fees, partial cancellation, voucher policy, time windows, jurisdiction). confidence.level=45, well below 65 threshold. trials.agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core lifecycle operation in logistics and courier systems. A user-initiated cancellation must respect the physical realities of fulfillment — once a package is in transit or delivered, cancellation becomes progressively harder or impossible. The PM's requirement is stated without any constraints on timing, order state, refund policy, or partial cancellation, making this a classically ambiguous feature request.","bounded_context":"Or
